title: Fatty Tender Mexican Beef Barbacoa
description: A fatty and tender Mexican dish from Veracruz, featuring beef and purple sprouting broccoli, perfect for a crowd. This recipe is Buddhist temple friendly and can be made in a barbacoa style.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat.
Sear the beef brisket until browned on all sides, then set aside.
Add the onion and garlic to the pot and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the chipotle peppers, cumin, oregano, lime juice, salt, and black pepper to the pot and stir to combine.
Add the browned beef brisket back to the pot and cover with a lid.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and braise for 3 hours.
After 2 hours, add the purple sprouting broccoli to the pot and continue to braise for another hour.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly before serving.
Shred the beef with two forks and serve with the broccoli and sauce.
